For pet owners and veterinary teams Demo only

The first read on a skin concern, before the appointment.

Add a few photos, mark what you are seeing, and get a plain-language concern profile you can copy into a message or bring to an appointment.

Try the checker
Possible matches, not diagnoses Vet-ready report export Client-side demo
Nurse review Book soon

Milo has been licking one damp patch since grooming on Monday.

04 photos and notes

What it does

The small details that make a vet call less vague.

Most pet skin calls start with half a story. PawDerm helps collect the parts clinics usually ask for: where it is, how long it has been there, what changed, and whether anything looks urgent.

Photo intake

Guide close-up, wider view, and alternate angle uploads so skin changes are easier to compare.

Urgency routing

Separate monitor, vet-soon, and urgent-vet paths with clear safety language.

Vet handoff

Generate a structured report with symptoms, duration, concern level, and next-step notes.

Follow-up history

Saved cases stay on the device so owners can compare what changed after a day or two.

Live product demo

Run the example, then make your own case note.

The sample demo shows the intended product feel. The upload checker below runs fully in the browser and saves cases locally on this device.

Example case

Milo, Labrador, damp patch after grooming

Lighting check Body area and timing Red-flag review Vet note
Ready Click Run example to create a sample concern note.

Clinic view

Morning skin concern queue

Urgent Miso, cat, swelling near eye Recommend same-day call
Vet soon Milo, dog, damp patch Photos clear, owner note ready
Monitor Juniper, dog, flaky skin Ask for repeat photo tomorrow

    Vet handoff

    Shareable report

    Create a note to generate a vet handoff summary.

    Care

    Call a vet

    Seek urgent care for severe pain, eye involvement, deep wounds, heavy bleeding, rapid swelling, fever, lethargy, or loss of appetite.

    Saved locally

    Case history

    How it fits

    A simple path from photo to call-ready summary.

    01

    Owner records the basics

    Pet type, body area, duration, symptoms, and notes become one organized case.

    02

    The tool separates red flags

    Severe signs are surfaced before less urgent possibilities, with conservative safety wording.

    03

    The clinic gets a clearer starting point

    The summary is written as context for a licensed veterinarian, not as a final answer.

    Pricing

    Simple plans for households and clinics.

    Pet Owner

    $9/mo

    For one household tracking occasional skin concerns.

    • Skin concern case notes
    • Vet handoff report export
    • Case history on device

    Pro Owner

    $29/mo

    For multi-pet homes, breeders, fosters, and frequent follow-ups.

    • Multi-pet case tracking concept
    • Photo quality guidance
    • Priority report templates

    Clinic

    Custom

    For teams that want better pre-visit skin concern intake.

    • Clinic queue concept
    • Staff review fields
    • Demo and integration planning

    Docs

    A practical way to collect skin concern details.

    These notes explain what PawDerm collects, how it flags risk, and how the report should be used.

    Photo intake

    POST /cases/photos

    Accept close-up and context images, then return quality notes for the owner.

    2. Triage

    GET /cases/:id/triage

    Separate severe signs from moderate symptoms while avoiding diagnosis language.

    Report export

    GET /cases/:id/report

    Generate a shareable text report for vet handoff and follow-up comparison.

    News and blog

    Short guides for better vet handoffs.

    Guide

    How to photograph a skin concern for a vet visit

    Close-up, wider view, natural light, and a dated follow-up photo can make the story clearer.

    Read article
    Clinic handoff

    Why possible matches are safer than instant diagnosis

    Many skin conditions look similar. Safer software organizes context and urgency, then points toward professional care.

    Read status
    Product

    Designing a calmer handoff between owners and clinics

    Better intake reduces vague calls and gives teams a structured place to start.

    View docs

    Regulatory status

    Built as triage support, not veterinary diagnosis.

    PawDerm AI is framed as educational intake support. Diagnosis, treatment, and prescribing belong to licensed veterinarians working within the appropriate veterinarian-client-patient relationship. The AVMA describes the VCPR as the basis for diagnosis, prescribing, and treatment.

    Animal skin conditions can look alike and often need veterinary examination, history, and diagnostic tests. PawDerm should help organize observations and urgency, then move the case toward professional care.

    AVMA VCPR reference Merck Veterinary Manual

    Prototype disclosure

    No prescription advice. No emergency replacement. No final diagnosis. Always contact a veterinarian for severe pain, eye involvement, bleeding, swelling, fever, lethargy, or loss of appetite.

    A browser-only demo for turning pet skin photos and notes into a clearer message for a vet.
